name: Hertfordshire (United Kingdom)
description: A blue field with a centred yellow stag (a hart) leaping out of water represented by white wavy lines beneath.
history: Designed by JR Loftus and chosen in a 2008 county-wide vote organised by BBC Three Counties Radio; registered with the Flag Institute. The hart-and-water motif puns on the county name (Hertford = 'hart's ford').

features.symbolism: the canting (punning) emblem of Hertford — 'hart's ford'
